tasks.description: Configure foundational documentation and minimal AI agent instructions.
acceptance_criteria: README.md exists at project root.
README.md contains project name 'Gymothy'.
README.md contains a clear value proposition.
README.md contains developer setup instructions for pnpm.
AGENTS.md exists at project root.
AGENTS.md describes 'Focus Mode' architecture.
AGENTS.md describes 'Exercise Shadowing' strategy.
AGENTS.md specifies Vitest + RTL as testing requirements.
CLAUDE.md exists as a symlink to AGENTS.md.
All boilerplate example code is removed from the app directory.

tasks.description: Set up Turso database with Drizzle ORM and implement the core schema.
acceptance_criteria: Drizzle ORM and @libsql/client are installed.
Drizzle-kit is installed as a dev dependency.
lib/db/schema.ts defines User table.
lib/db/schema.ts defines Exercise table with external_id and user_id.
lib/db/schema.ts defines Workout table.
lib/db/schema.ts defines WorkoutExerciseGroup table.
lib/db/schema.ts defines WorkoutExercise table.
lib/db/schema.ts defines WorkoutSet table.
lib/db/schema.ts defines feedback enum ('too_easy', 'solid', 'struggle', 'failure').
lib/db/schema.ts defines Routine, RoutineGroup, RoutineExercise, and RoutineSet tables.
drizzle.config.ts is configured for libsql.
pnpm drizzle-kit generate creates migration files.
pnpm drizzle-kit push succeeds against local SQLite file.

tasks.description: Implement Better Auth with Email/Password provider and Drizzle adapter.
acceptance_criteria: better-auth and @better-auth/drizzle-adapter are installed.
lib/auth.ts initialized with Drizzle adapter.
emailAndPassword provider enabled in lib/auth.ts.
app/api/auth/[...all]/route.ts handler exists.
middleware.ts protects private routes (History, Routines, Workout).
Better Auth schema successfully generated via CLI.
Endpoint /api/ok returns {status: 'ok'}.
.env.example contains BETTER_AUTH_SECRET.
.env.example contains BETTER_AUTH_URL.

tasks.description: Configure Playwright, Vitest and React Testing Library for automated verification.
acceptance_criteria: Playwright is set up using `pnpm create playwright`.
vitest and @testing-library/react are installed.
vitest.config.ts is correctly configured for Next.js.
tests/setup.ts exists.
tests/setup.ts handles JSDOM global setup.
tests/setup.ts handles React Testing Library cleanup.
pnpm test command executes test suite.
pnpm test:e2e command executes Playwright tests.
Dummy unit test passes.
Dummy component test passes.
Dummy e2e test passes in Playwright.
README.md is updated with testing instructions.

tasks.description: Configure the project for Vercel deployment.
acceptance_criteria: pnpm build succeeds locally without lint/type errors.
next.config.ts defaults are appropriate for Vercel.
.env.example includes all keys required for production.
Application landing page loads on Vercel deployment URL.

tasks.description: Integrate ExerciseDB API with MSW mocking for testing.
acceptance_criteria: msw is installed.
tests/mocks/handlers.ts defines ExerciseDB mock responses.
tests/mocks/server.ts configures MSW for Node.
Service/hook for fetching ExerciseDB data exists.
API fetching handles non-200 status codes.
API fetching handles network timeouts.
Unit tests for fetching logic pass with MSW.

tasks.description: Build mobile-optimized UI components for browsing exercises.
acceptance_criteria: Exercise Card displays movement name.
Exercise Card displays target muscle group.
Exercise Card displays body part.
Exercise Card displays equipment.
Exercise Card contains GIF/placeholder area.
Exercise List handles empty search results.
Exercise List displays loading skeleton during fetch.
Clickable UI elements in list are minimum 2rem height.

tasks.description: Implement the logic to search and filter both Global and Custom exercises.
acceptance_criteria: Search results aggregate API and local DB data.
Search input is debounced.
Filtering by Body Part is functional.
Filtering by Equipment is functional.
Filtering by Target Muscle is functional.
Filter options are derived from dataset.
Custom exercises are visually distinguished from Global ones.

tasks.description: Implement Custom Exercise management using Tanstack Form and Server Actions.
acceptance_criteria: @tanstack/react-form and zod are installed.
Zod schema defines exercise validation rules.
createCustomExercise Server Action persists data.
updateCustomExercise Server Action modifies existing data.
deleteCustomExercise Server Action removes data.
Form displays real-time validation errors.
Form prevents duplicate names for the same user.
Muscle/Equipment selectors are functional.

tasks.description: Implement the state-driven logic for drafting a routine.
acceptance_criteria: Zustand store manages routine draft state.
Action to add exercise to draft exists.
Action to remove exercise from draft exists.
Draft state mirrors to localStorage.
Reloading page restores routine draft from localStorage.
Cancel action prompts for confirmation on unsaved changes.

tasks.description: Implement the UI and logic for grouping exercises into supersets within the builder.
acceptance_criteria: Action to group 2+ exercises exists.
UI displays visual container/box for supersets.
Action to reorder exercises within a group exists.
Action to reorder groups within the routine exists.
Reorder UI uses mobile-friendly handles or buttons.

tasks.description: Implement the persistence logic for saving routines and shadowing global exercises.
acceptance_criteria: saveRoutine Server Action persists routine hierarchy.
Global exercises are shadowed to local DB upon saving.
Shadowing logic skips already-shadowed external_ids.
Target Weight/Reps/Duration map correctly to RoutineSet table.
RoutineGroup and RoutineExercise join records are created correctly.

tasks.description: Implement the library list and blueprint detail views for routines.
acceptance_criteria: Routines tab displays user's routines.
Routine list item shows name and exercise count.
Detail view displays Groups -> Exercises -> Targets hierarchy.
Detail view displays persistent exercise notes.
Delete flow is accessible from Routine Detail.
Edit flow is accessible from Routine Detail.

tasks.description: Implement the Zustand store for managing active workout state.
acceptance_criteria: useWorkoutSession Zustand store is implemented.
Store manages a hierarchical state of WorkoutGroups, Exercises, and Sets.
Store provides a 'startWorkout' action that accepts a Routine template.
Store provides a 'startWorkout' action that initializes an empty template.
Store maintains a 'lastUpdated' timestamp for inactivity tracking.

tasks.description: Implement the localStorage persistence and time-based resumption logic.
acceptance_criteria: Workout session state is synchronized to localStorage on every change.
App detects active session in localStorage upon initialization.
Utility logic identifies sessions < 2 hours old as 'active'.
Utility logic identifies sessions 2-4 hours old as 'stale'.
Utility logic identifies sessions > 4 hours old as 'abandoned'.
Initial page load auto-redirects 'active' sessions to the workout screen.
Redirect logic executes before dashboard render to prevent content flashing.

tasks.description: Build the layout shell for the Focus Mode experience.
acceptance_criteria: Focus Mode layout suppresses the global BottomTabBar.
Top-level ProgressBar reflects (completed groups / total groups) * 100.
Toggle button exists to switch between Carousel and List views.
Finish button triggers a session summary overlay.
Discard button triggers a confirmation dialog.

tasks.description: Implement the horizontal Carousel component for Focus Mode.
acceptance_criteria: Carousel renders exactly one WorkoutExerciseGroup card at a time.
Carousel container uses CSS scroll-snap for card alignment.
Superset groups render interleaved exercise rows within the card.
Large 'Previous' and 'Next' navigation buttons are fixed to the UI.
Swiping gesture updates the active card index in the session store.

tasks.description: Implement the set-logging interaction layer.
acceptance_criteria: Weight input uses inputmode='decimal' for mobile keyboards.
Reps input uses inputmode='numeric' for mobile keyboards.
Input fields and checkboxes have a minimum 2rem hit box.
Tapping a checkbox marks the set 'completed' in Zustand store.
Inputs are pre-filled with values from the user's previous session for that movement.
Buttons to add/remove sets from the active exercise are functional.

tasks.description: Implement the automatic feedback overlay and auto-advance logic.
acceptance_criteria: Feedback overlay triggers upon checking the final set of a card.
Overlay displays buttons for 'Too Easy', 'Solid', 'Struggle', 'Failure'.
Selecting an option updates the feedback field of the last set in the card.
Selecting an option triggers a carousel advance to the next card.
Feedback overlay is non-modal and does not block manual swiping.

tasks.description: Implement the reactive rest timer functionality.
acceptance_criteria: Timer countdown initializes automatically when any set is marked completed.
Timer UI is displayed as a non-intrusive floating pill.
Timer UI provides a '+30s' action button.
Timer UI provides a 'Skip' action button.
Visual indicator (e.g., flashing or color change) triggers when timer hits zero.

tasks.description: Implement the primary history tab and chronological workout list.
acceptance_criteria: History tab is set as the default landing page for authenticated users.
Past workouts are displayed in a chronological list, most recent first.
Each list item displays the workout name and start time.
Each list item displays the total volume lifted during that session.
Each list item shows a summarized list of exercises performed.
Empty state correctly prompts the user to start their first workout.

tasks.description: Implement the UI components for session status and active session resumption.
acceptance_criteria: Workouts with >4h inactivity display an 'Incomplete' badge in the history list.
Tapping an 'Incomplete' workout opens the workout in Overview (Edit) mode.
A 'Resume Workout' banner appears on the History tab for sessions with 2-4h inactivity.
The banner 'Resume' button launches the session in Focus Mode.
The banner 'Finish' button saves the session as-is and returns to the default History state.

tasks.description: Implement the GitHub-style contribution grid for volume and effort visualization.
acceptance_criteria: Heatmap grid renders at the top of the History tab.
Default heatmap state reflects daily total volume lifted.
A toggle switch exists to alternate between 'Volume' and 'Effort' modes.
In 'Effort' mode, color intensity reflects the average qualitative feedback for that day.
Legend and tooltips provide context for color intensity across modes.
Grid correctly spans the last 12 months of user activity.

tasks.description: Implement the ability to modify past workout data.
acceptance_criteria: Navigation from history list opens a full edit view of the past workout.
Users can add or remove sets from any exercise in a completed workout.
Users can modify weight, reps, and feedback values for any past set.
Saving changes triggers a re-calculation of the workout's total volume.
A destructive 'Delete Workout' action is available with a confirmation dialog.

tasks.description: Implement the deterministic logic for volume progression recommendations.
acceptance_criteria: A utility function 'calculateProgression' is implemented and tested.
Logic suggests adjustments to weight/reps to achieve target volume increase.
Logic constraints compound exercise suggestions to 6-10 reps.
Logic constraints isolation exercise suggestions to 8-12 reps.
'too_easy' feedback triggers a ~5-10% suggested volume increase.
'solid' feedback triggers a ~2.5-5% suggested volume increase.
'struggle' feedback results in no volume change (maintenance).
'failure' feedback results in no volume change or a suggested decrease.
Unit tests cover all feedback paths and adjustment edge cases.

tasks.description: Integrate progression suggestions into the Active Workout logging experience.
acceptance_criteria: Active Workout inputs are initialized with targets from the Progression Engine.
Targets are derived from the user's most recent session for each exercise.
Suggested values are displayed as distinct 'placeholder' or 'ghost' text.
Engine correctly identifies 'Discovery' mode for exercises with no history.
Discovery mode defaults to blank inputs or sensible baseline targets.

tasks.description: Implement the first-run experience and empty states.
acceptance_criteria: Empty Routines tab displays a 'Create your first Routine' call-to-action.
Empty History tab displays a 'How it works' summary for new users.
A non-blocking introductory overlay explains Carousel navigation during the first workout session.
User's 'First Run' status is persisted to ensure onboarding only shows once.

tasks.description: Add micro-interactions and transitions to enhance the user experience.
acceptance_criteria: Carousel cards use performant CSS or Framer Motion transitions for swiping and navigation.
Checking off a set triggers a visual completion animation.
Switching between primary tabs and Focus Mode is smoothly animated.
Workout completion triggers a high-energy celebratory animation or effect.

tasks.description: Perform a final audit and polish of the user interface.
acceptance_criteria: Color contrast is audited and adjusted for readability in bright sunlight.
Every actionable UI element meets the minimum 44x44px touch target requirement.
Skeleton loading screens are implemented for all primary data-fetching views.
Input focus management is optimized to prevent unexpected mobile keyboard behavior.

tasks.description: Set up a high-fidelity Vitest environment using an in-memory LibSQL database (Turso dialect) and global Drizzle mocking to ensure full compatibility with Turso-specific migrations.
acceptance_criteria: Vitest dev dependencies (vitest, @libsql/client, @faker-js/faker) are installed.
vitest.config.ts is configured with a 'setupFiles' entry for database initialization.
A 'tests/setup.ts' file exists to manage the test lifecycle.
An in-memory LibSQL client is initialized using 'createClient({ url: ":memory:" })'.
A Drizzle 'testDb' instance is created using the 'drizzle-orm/libsql' adapter and project schema.
The production '@/lib/db' module is globally intercepted using 'vi.mock' to provide 'testDb'.
Database migrations from './drizzle' are successfully applied to the in-memory DB in a 'beforeAll' hook using the LibSQL migrator.
A 'clearDb' utility is implemented using raw SQL to truncate all schema tables while respecting/disabling foreign key constraints temporarily.
The 'clearDb' utility is executed in a 'beforeEach' hook to ensure total test isolation.
An exercise factory utility is created in 'tests/factories/exercise.factory.ts' using '@faker-js/faker'.
The exercise factory supports 'Partial' overrides to allow tests to control specific fields.
A test suite for 'getCustomExercises' is implemented verifying 'userId' isolation.
A test suite for 'getCustomExercises' is implemented verifying 'search' partial matching.
A test suite for 'getCustomExercises' is implemented verifying 'bodyParts' array/OR logic.
A test suite for 'getCustomExercises' is implemented verifying 'limit' and 'offset' pagination.

tasks.description: Implement foundational routing and root layout to resolve orphaned UI components.
acceptance_criteria: app/layout.tsx is created at the root of the app directory.
app/layout.tsx includes the standard <html> and <body> tags.
app/layout.tsx imports and applies app/globals.css.
app/layout.tsx renders the {children} prop within the body.
app/layout.tsx exports a 'metadata' object with title 'Gymothy' and a description.
app/page.tsx is created at the root of the app directory.
app/page.tsx renders a functional landing page component.
app/page.tsx is accessible at the root URL (/).
Browser tab title matches the metadata provided in the root layout.

tasks.description: Implement Next.js middleware for route protection and session validation.
acceptance_criteria: middleware.ts is created at the project root.
middleware.ts correctly imports the auth instance from lib/auth.ts.
middleware.ts utilizes the better-auth/next-js middleware utility.
middleware.ts prevents access to /exercises for unauthenticated users.
middleware.ts prevents access to /routines for unauthenticated users.
middleware.ts prevents access to /history for unauthenticated users.
middleware.ts allows public access to the root URL (/).
middleware.ts allows public access to auth API routes (/api/auth/*).
Unauthenticated attempts to access protected routes redirect to the landing page.

tasks.description: Implement the client-side authentication instance to enable frontend session management.
acceptance_criteria: lib/auth-client.ts is created in the lib directory.
lib/auth-client.ts exports a singleton 'authClient' instance.
authClient is initialized using the 'createAuthClient' utility.
authClient baseURL is configured correctly via environment variables.
A client component can successfully import authClient and use the 'useSession' hook.
Authentication state (isLoggedIn/User) is reactive within React components.

tasks.description: Refactor UI components to support stable refs and React 19 forwardRef patterns.
acceptance_criteria: components/ui/button.tsx is refactored to use React.forwardRef.
components/ui/input.tsx is refactored to use React.forwardRef.
components/ui/card.tsx subcomponents are refactored to use React.forwardRef.
components/ui/badge.tsx is refactored to use React.forwardRef.
components/ui/skeleton.tsx is refactored to use React.forwardRef.
components/ui/spinner.tsx is refactored to use React.forwardRef.
Each refactored component correctly passes the 'ref' to its underlying DOM element.
Each refactored component correctly types the 'ref' in its TypeScript interface.
Component tests (e.g., button.test.tsx) verify that refs can be successfully attached.

tasks.description: Optimize interactive UI components for mobile touch targets (44px minimum).
acceptance_criteria: buttonVariants in components/ui/button.tsx is updated for accessible sizing.
The 'default' size for Button is increased to a minimum height of 44px (e.g., h-11).
The 'icon' size for Button is increased to a minimum width/height of 44px (e.g., size-11).
Input component base height in components/ui/input.tsx is increased to 44px.
All interactive variants (sm, lg) are adjusted to ensure mobile touch targets are >= 44px.
A visual regression or manual check confirms layout remains consistent after size changes.
Accessibility tests verify that all interactive elements meet WCAG touch target standards.

tasks.description: Refactor ExerciseCard to use semantic HTML and correct Base UI primitives.
acceptance_criteria: components/exercise-card.tsx root element is changed from 'div' to a native 'button'.
Alternatively, components/exercise-card.tsx uses the 'Button' primitive from components/ui/button.tsx.
Manual role='button' and tabIndex={0} attributes are removed.
Manual Enter/Space keydown handlers are removed (rely on native button behavior).
The component maintains its existing visual styling and layout.
The component continues to correctly trigger the 'onClick' prop when activated.
The component correctly satisfies the 44px minimum height requirement.
exercise-card.test.tsx is updated to assert the root element is a 'button'.

tasks.description: Move exercise search and filter state into URL search parameters for navigation persistence.
acceptance_criteria: lib/exercises/use-exercises.ts is refactored to initialize state from URL searchParams.
The hook uses 'useSearchParams' or 'usePathname' from next/navigation.
Updating the search query triggers a URL update (e.g., ?q=bench).
Updating filters (bodyPart, equipment, target) triggers corresponding URL updates.
URL updates use 'router.push' or 'router.replace' to manage browser history.
Page reload successfully restores all filter and search states from the URL.
Clearing a filter correctly removes the parameter from the URL.
Local 'useState' for these specific filter values is removed from the hook.

tasks.description: Implement a server-side proxy for ExerciseDB API calls to enhance security and caching.
acceptance_criteria: A Next.js Route Handler (e.g., /api/exercises) is created to proxy ExerciseDB requests.
lib/exercises/service.ts is refactored to fetch from the internal proxy instead of ExerciseDB directly.
External API keys or sensitive logic are moved from the client to the server proxy.
The proxy correctly forwards search, filter, and pagination parameters to ExerciseDB.
The proxy implements server-side caching (e.g., unstable_cache or fetch options) where appropriate.
The proxy handles ExerciseDB error responses and returns semantic HTTP status codes.
Unit tests for the proxy route verify correct data forwarding and error handling.

tasks.description: Standardize CVA and className merging patterns across all UI components.
acceptance_criteria: The 'Button' component refactored to use the explicit cn(variants(...), className) pattern.
The 'EmptyMedia' component refactored to use the explicit cn(variants(...), className) pattern.
All components in components/ui follow the same pattern for merging external classNames.
All components in components/ui utilize the 'cn' utility consistently.
A manual review confirms that variant-specific styles are correctly applied to all components.
Existing component tests continue to pass without style regressions.

tasks.description: Implement runtime environment variable validation using Zod.
acceptance_criteria: A validation schema for environment variables is created (e.g., using Zod).
Required variables like BETTER_AUTH_SECRET and BETTER_AUTH_URL are included in the schema.
Database connection strings and API URLs are included in the schema.
The application fails to start (throws error) if required variables are missing or malformed.
An env.ts (or similar) utility is created to export validated environment variables.
Existing code is updated to import variables from the validated utility instead of process.env.
Development and production environments are both covered by the validation.

tasks.description: Refactor Server Actions to return structured result objects instead of throwing errors.
acceptance_criteria: lib/exercises/actions.ts actions are refactored to return a common Result type.
The Result type includes fields for 'success' (boolean), 'data' (generic), and 'error' (string).
Actions handle internal errors gracefully and return an error result instead of crashing.
Client components (e.g., useExercises) are updated to handle the new structured response.
Type safety is maintained for the 'data' payload in successful results.
A helper utility (e.g., 'toResult') is considered to standardize the conversion.
Unit tests verify that actions return the correct success/error structure.

tasks.description: Perform code hygiene cleanup by removing unused imports and redundant configurations.
acceptance_criteria: Unused 'use' import is removed from lib/exercises/types.ts.
Redundant 'dotenv/config' import is removed from lib/db/index.ts.
A project-wide lint check (pnpm lint) passes without errors.
A project-wide format check (pnpm format) passes without changes.
Dead code or placeholder files are identified and removed.
All remaining code follows the project's established style and conventions.

tasks.description: Improve getCustomExercises query robustness by adding deterministic sorting and robust JSON array searching.
acceptance_criteria: getCustomExercises includes an explicit ORDER BY clause (e.g., updatedAt DESC) for stable pagination.
JSON array searching for bodyParts, equipments, and targetMuscles is refactored to use more robust matching (e.g., JSON_EACH or exact bounds) to prevent partial word matches.
Unit tests verify that pagination remains stable across multiple calls.
Unit tests verify that searching for 'Chest' does not match 'Chestnut'.
